name: Combo Chain
description: A sequence system where attacks can link into later actions through timing, hit state, input windows, or cancel rules.
design_notes: Combo chains define combat rhythm and skill ceiling. A chain should specify when the next input is accepted, whether links require hit confirmation, which actions cancel recovery, and how repeated attacks avoid becoming an infinite loop. The strongest combo systems expose rules that players can feel without reading data tables.
design_purpose: Let players express mastery through planned attack sequences.
Create weapon or character identity through branching routes and cancel rules.
Reward successful hits with better follow-up options while preserving defensive escapes.
player_fantasy: The player feels stylish and intentional, building pressure through a sequence they chose.

parameters.name: combo_input_buffer_frames
parameters.description: Frames before a link window where the next input is accepted.

parameters.name: link_open_frame
parameters.description: First frame where the next combo step may start.

parameters.name: link_close_frame
parameters.description: Last frame where the next combo step may start.

parameters.name: hit_confirm_required
parameters.description: Whether the next step requires the current attack to connect.

parameters.name: branch_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule table entry used to select the next action branch.

parameters.name: repeat_scaling_ratio
parameters.description: Damage or stun multiplier applied to repeated chain hits.

edge_cases: Inputs during hit stop, pause, slow motion, or rollback need one authoritative buffer clock.
Whiffed attacks should define whether they can chain, only recover, or cancel into defense.
Changing weapons or targets mid-chain must clear or translate the current combo index.
Airborne and grounded chain routes should not accidentally share invalid next steps.
common_bugs: Queued input triggers twice because both animation event and state update consume it.
Combo index fails to reset after interruption and the next attack starts at step three.
Attack cancel metadata differs between client and server, causing desync in networked combat.
A late input is accepted visually but rejected by damage state, making the combo feel inconsistent.
balancing_notes: Long chains need escape valves such as pushback, resource limits, decay, or defensive cancels.
High-damage finishers should require commitment, positioning, or hit confirmation.
accessibility_notes: Support input buffering and readable cancel windows rather than requiring exact frame-perfect presses for basic chains.
Use training or tutorial prompts to expose combo routes without relying on hidden notation.

implementation_notes.unity: Model combat timing and hit resolution as data-owned state, drive animation from that state, and apply damage through one authoritative resolver rather than animation-only callbacks.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep combat windows on the actor state, evaluate Area2D/Area3D or shape queries during physics ticks, and emit presentation signals after authoritative damage resolution.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Gameplay Ability System, Actor Components, or replicated combat state for timing and damage authority, with montage notifies only marking windows or presentation events.
implementation_notes.web: Resolve combat in the fixed simulation step from actor state, hit volumes, and attack metadata, then queue UI/audio/camera events from the confirmed result.

example_games.title: Devil May Cry
example_games.note: Rewards varied attack sequences and expressive chaining.

example_games.title: Bayonetta
example_games.note: Uses combo strings as a major combat expression tool.
